pr.title: Decompile stub functions across it/, gr/, gm/, ft/, lb/, and mn/ modules
pr.author: stephenjayakar

summary: Large first-pass decompilation PR that replaced many `/// #...` stubs across item, stage, fighter, library, game-mode, and menu code with matching C implementations, while updating headers, signatures, and item/stage/fighter struct layouts needed by those matches. The PR body claims 79 previously stubbed functions were decompiled, dominated by item callbacks and handlers, with smaller stage, fighter, lbMthp, and mnname changes. Review focused on avoiding bad m2c artifacts, fake overlay casts, inline asm matches, raw offset/flag constants, and missing ItemVar structs.
what_changed: Decompiled many item stubs, with the PR excerpt stating 72 item functions across files such as `itfreeze`, `itwstar`, `itpikachutjoltground`, `itrshell`, `itpatapata`, `itgamewatchchef`, `ittaru`, `itevyoshiegg`, `itbombhei`, `itseakchain`, `itwhitebea`, `itnesspkfire`, `itsamuschargeshot`, `itcrazyhandbomb`, `itgreatfoxlaser`, `ithouou`, `itkabigon`, `itkyasarin`, `itlikelike`, `itoldkuri`, `ittools`, `itunknown`, `itpikachuthunder`, and `itmewtwoshadowball`.

Expanded item variable and attribute definitions in `itCommonItems.h` and `itCharItems.h`, including structures or fields for `itPikachutJoltGround_ItemVars`, `itPikachutJoltGroundAttributes`, `itSamusChargeshot_ItemVars`, `itGamewatchchef_ItemVars`, `itGamewatchchefAttributes`, `itTools_ItemVars`, `itToolsAttributes`, `itWstar_ItemVars`, `itWstarAttributes`, `itFreeze_ItemVars`, `itLikelike_ItemVars`, `itHitodeman_ItemVars`, `itTaru_ItemVars`, `itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars`, and related Pokemon/item attrs shown in the diff excerpt.

Implemented fighter stubs including `fn_800DB8A4` in `ftCo_Attack100.c`, Crazy Hand routines such as `ftCh_Slap_Phys` and `fn_801587B0`, Kirby special-N routines such as `ftKb_SpecialN_800F15D8` and `fn_800F64C8`, and `ft_80089824` in `ft_0892.c`.

Updated fighter motion-variable layout in `ft/chara/ftCommon/types.h`, replacing capture-wait padding with concrete fields `x4` and `x8` so `fn_800DB8A4` could use `fp->mv.co.capturewait` instead of a fake overlay cast.

Updated function signatures in headers, including `ft_80089460(Fighter*)`, `fn_800F64C8(Fighter_GObj*, float)`, `InitializePersistentNameData(s32)`, `grMuteCity_801F0F4C(Ground_GObj*)`, and `grOnett_801E5140(s32 idx)`.

Decompiled stage stubs in `grhomerun`, `gricemt`, `grmutecity`, `gronett`, `grpura`, and `grrcruise`, including repeated Home Run stage setup patterns, Ice Mountain variable accesses, Mute City F-Zero car setup, Onett table dispatch, Pura toon texture setup, and Rainbow Cruise JObj visibility flag handling.
Expanded stage variable structs in `gr/types.h`, including `grIceMt_GroundVars` fields `x10C` and `x10E` and splitting `grMuteCity_GroundVars` around `xC4/xC6/xC8` to match narrower fields.
Implemented two lbMthp functions according to the PR excerpt and fixed an `HSD_SObj` return type; exact function bodies are not present in the provided diff excerpt.
Implemented `CreateNameAtIndex` in `mn/mnname.c` and adjusted `InitializePersistentNameData` declaration in `gmmain_lib.h`; exact menu body details are not shown in the excerpt.
smart_moves: Used new struct fields and typed headers to replace raw m2c offset accesses where review identified better layouts, for example moving `fn_800DB8A4` from a fake `FighterOverlay` cast to `fp->mv.co.capturewait` and named `p_ftCommonData` fields.
Accepted review feedback on `gricemt` by replacing a raw `(u8*) gp + 0x10E` style access with `&gp->gv.icemt.x10E`, backed by a `grIceMt_GroundVars` struct update.
Used symbolic constants for JObj flags after review: `grRCruise_801FF73C` uses `JOBJ_HIDDEN` rather than raw `0x10`.
Removed or avoided at least one bad inline assembly match after review questioned why `itNessYoyo` generated asm; author acknowledged it was bad and said the match would be removed.
Converted broad `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations to typed signatures when implementations made argument and return types clear.

Recognized repeated stage initialization patterns, such as `grHomeRun_8021DEF0`, `grHomeRun_8021E074`, `grHomeRun_8021E1F8`, and `grHomeRun_8021E37C` all doing `grAnime_801C8138`, clearing a ground var, and storing `Ground_801C3FA4(gobj, 1)`.
decomp_lessons: Do not trust m2c casts blindly. Reviewer feedback specifically called out useless casts and asked whether `--valid-syntax` was used; the final `ftCo_Attack100.c` excerpt is cleaner and avoids unnecessary `f64`, `u8*`, and fake overlay casts.

Avoid fake overlay structs when the data is actually a known motion-variable union or fighter substructure. For `fn_800DB8A4`, `FighterOverlay*` was reviewed as a bad pattern and replaced with `fp->mv.co.capturewait` fields.
Inline asm inside a decompilation PR is a red flag unless intentionally justified. The reviewed `itNessYoyo` asm match was called out and the author agreed to remove it.
When a raw offset appears stable, prefer adding the correct field to the owning struct and referencing it directly, as with `gp->gv.icemt.x10E` and capturewait fields.
Prefer existing constants/macros over magic values, demonstrated by replacing raw `0x10` JObj flag usage with `JOBJ_HIDDEN`.
First-pass matches need not over-polish all helper macros. The `ABS` macro suggestion for `itfreeze` was acknowledged as real but not required for a first-pass match.

If an item uses bytes in `ip->xDD4_itemVar`, add or extend an item-specific ItemVar struct instead of writing through anonymous `M2C_FIELD` accesses; review explicitly requested a new ItemVar struct if necessary for `itgreatfoxlaser`.
Be cautious when reusing existing item variable unions from unrelated items. Review flagged `itlugia` using `foxblaster` fields as probably wrong: 'Not foxblaster'.
Repeated tiny functions may share an inline; review suggested this for several `itevyoshiegg` damage/event handlers.
naming_conventions: Item variable structs follow `it<Name>_ItemVars`, for example `itFreeze_ItemVars`, `itWstar_ItemVars`, `itTaru_ItemVars`, `itGamewatchchef_ItemVars`, and `itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars`.

Item attribute structs follow `it<Name>Attributes` or item-specific variants such as `itWstarAttributes`, `itPikachutJoltGroundAttributes`, `itGamewatchchefAttributes`, `itToolsAttributes`, and `itLugiaAttributes`.
Attribute array entries are named with an `AttrEntry` suffix, as in `itWstarAttrEntry`, `itGamewatchchefAttrEntry`, and `itToolsAttrEntry`.
Stage ground variable structs use `gr<Stage>_GroundVars`, such as `grIceMt_GroundVars` and `grMuteCity_GroundVars`.

For still-unknown fields, this PR continues the local decomp style of offset-like names such as `xDD4`, `x10E`, `xC4`, `x2340`, and `x3A8`, but the review encourages placing them in the correct owner struct rather than accessing raw offsets.
Function signatures in headers are upgraded from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete return and parameter types as soon as the implementation establishes them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma dont_inline on` around at least `fn_800DB8A4`, indicating a matching tactic to prevent compiler inlining in a small fighter helper.
Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in some matches such as `fn_802130D0` and `it_8028F434`, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for matching.
Reviewer guidance: pass `--valid-syntax` to m2c to avoid useless or misleading casts in generated C.
Reviewer guidance: do not accept generated inline asm as a normal decomp result; an `itNessYoyo` asm attempt was considered a bad match and removed.

For raw data accesses produced by m2c, convert to typed fields and named constants where possible: `p_ftCommonData->grab_timer_decrement`, `p_ftCommonData->x3A8`, `fp->mv.co.capturewait.x0/x8`, `&gp->gv.icemt.x10E`, and `JOBJ_HIDDEN` are examples from the final excerpt.

review_feedback.feedback: Ribbanya said the casts in the m2c-derived match were not useful and asked whether `--valid-syntax` was being passed to m2c. Author replied they were not, tried it, and later said it was fixed.

BR- said the `FighterOverlay` cast was fake and changes should be made to `fp->mv.co` instead. Ribbanya later called the overlay pattern very bad and said it needed removal. Author replied that it was moved to `fp->mv.co`.

Ribbanya questioned why the match generated asm. Author agreed it was bad, removed that match, and said they would watch for bad matches like it in the future. The final changed-file list in the provided context does not include `itnessyoyo.c`, supporting that this was likely removed from the final PR.
BR- suggested replacing a raw offset-style pointer with `&gp->x10E` or similar. The final excerpt uses `&gp->gv.icemt.x10E` and adds that field to `grIceMt_GroundVars`.
BR- noted that raw flag `0x10` is `JOBJ_HIDDEN`; final excerpt uses `JOBJ_HIDDEN` in `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll` and `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll`.
BR- suggested several handlers were probably sharing the same inline.
BR- suggested an absolute-value operation might use the `ABS` macro; Ribbanya agreed it was likely but said it was not necessary for a first-pass match.
BR- requested adding a new ItemVar struct if necessary instead of leaving anonymous item-var field access.
BR- flagged reuse of `foxblaster` item-var fields for Lugia as wrong: 'Not foxblaster'.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for removal or replacement of fake `FighterOverlay` patterns in fighter code.
Search item files for remaining `M2C_FIELD(&ip->xDD4_itemVar._` accesses that should become typed ItemVar structs.
Search for remaining raw `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll(..., 0x10)` or `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll(..., 0x10)` uses that should be `JOBJ_HIDDEN`.
Search for remaining `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO` inline asm matches in item files, especially around `itNessYoyo`.
Inspect follow-up changes to `itlugia` to see whether foxblaster item-var reuse was replaced with a Lugia-specific struct.
Search for handlers similar to `itevyoshiegg` damage/event callbacks to identify shared inline helpers.
Search for `ABS` macro use in item physics code to standardize first-pass absolute-value patterns when matching permits.
